description: Skill Management - Add skill lifecycle management (list, install, remove) to purr-cli for multi-agent skill discovery and installation from Pieverse Marketplace

userStories.title: Agent registry and directory detection
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an agent registry that defines supported agents, their skill directory paths, and auto-detection logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/skill/agents.ts with agent definitions

Define agents: OpenClaw (local: .openclaw/workspace/skills/<slug>, global: ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/<slug>), Claude Code (.claude/skills/<slug>), Cursor (.cursor/skills/<slug>), Windsurf (.windsurf/skills/<slug>), Cline (.cline/skills/<slug>), GitHub Copilot (.github/copilot/skills/<slug>)
Each agent has: name, slug, localSkillPath(projectRoot, slug) function, globalSkillPath(slug) function
Export detectInstalled() that checks which agents exist on the current system by probing their config directories
detectInstalled() returns array of agent definitions that are present
Export getAgent(name) to look up a single agent by slug
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundation module. No external dependencies needed. Use Node.js fs.existsSync for detection.

userStories.title: Lock file CRUD module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a lock file system to persist skill installation state for reproducibility.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/skill/lock.ts

Define LockEntry type: { slug: string, name: string, sha256: string, installed_at: string, install_method: 'symlink' | 'copy', canonical_path: string, agent_installs: Record<string, { path: string, method: 'symlink' | 'copy' }> }
Define LockFile type: { skills: LockEntry[] }
Project-level lock file path: ./skills-lock.json
Global-level lock file path: ~/.purrfectclaw/skills-lock.json
Export readLock(scope: 'local' | 'global'): returns LockFile, returns empty { skills: [] } if file does not exist
Export writeLock(scope, lockFile): atomic write (write to .tmp then rename)
Export upsertLockEntry(scope, entry): add or update a skill entry
Export removeLockEntry(scope, slug): remove a skill entry by slug
Export getLockEntry(scope, slug): get a single entry or undefined
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundation module. Use Node.js fs for file ops. Atomic write prevents corruption on interrupted writes.

userStories.title: Marketplace API client
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an API client to interact with the Pieverse Skill Marketplace REST API.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/skill/api.ts
Export listSkills(options?: { search?: string, category?: string, offset?: number, limit?: number }): fetches GET /skills with query params, returns { skills: SkillMeta[], total: number }
SkillMeta type: { slug: string, name: string, description: string, category: string, download_url: string }
Export getSkill(slug: string): fetches GET /skills/{slug}, returns full skill detail
Export downloadSkill(slug: string): fetches GET /skills/{slug}/download, returns { buffer: Buffer, sha256: string } where sha256 comes from X-Skill-SHA256 response header
Base URL configurable via SKILL_MARKETPLACE_URL env var, fallback to loadConfig() for 'skill-marketplace-url' key, then hardcoded default
Reuse loadConfig pattern from existing src/api-client.ts for config reading
Clear error messages for network errors, 404, and non-200 responses
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Uses native fetch (Node 18+). No auth needed - public API. Reference src/api-client.ts for config pattern.

userStories.title: ZIP installer module (download, extract, symlink/copy)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an installer that downloads skill ZIPs, extracts them, and places them in agent directories.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/skill/installer.ts
Export extractZip(buffer: Buffer, targetDir: string): extracts ZIP to target directory
Validate all paths during extraction to prevent zip-slip / path traversal attacks (reject entries with .. or absolute paths)
Export installSkill(options: { slug, buffer, sha256, scope, agents, copyMode }): orchestrates full install flow
installSkill writes to canonical path: local = .skills/<slug>/, global = ~/.purrfectclaw/skills/<slug>/
Default symlink mode: extract to canonical, create symlink from each agent skill dir to canonical
Copy mode (--copy): extract directly to each agent skill dir, no symlinks
If symlink creation fails (e.g. Windows), auto-fallback to copy mode for that agent
Verify SHA256 of downloaded buffer matches expected hash, throw on mismatch
Return install result with actual method used per agent for lock file recording
Export uninstallSkill(options: { slug, scope, agentSlug? }): remove symlinks/copies and clean up canonical if no agents remain
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use fflate or built-in ZIP parsing for extraction. Use Node.js crypto for SHA256. Import agents from agents.ts and lock from lock.ts.

userStories.title: Skill command routing in main.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the 'skill' command group wired into the existing CLI router.
acceptanceCriteria: Add 'skill' case to the main switch statement in src/main.ts
Sub-commands: list, install, remove
Route to dynamic imports: src/skill/commands/list.ts, src/skill/commands/install.ts, src/skill/commands/remove.ts
Create stub files for each command that export a handler function accepting parsed args
Add 'skill' to the help text output with description of available sub-commands
Unknown sub-command prints help for skill group
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow existing routing pattern in main.ts (switch on group, then switch on command). Stub handlers can just print 'not implemented yet'.

userStories.title: List remote skills command
userStories.description: As an agent operator, I want to list available skills from the marketplace so I can discover what to install.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement src/skill/commands/list.ts handler for --remote mode
purr skill list --remote calls listSkills() from api.ts and displays results
Each skill shows: slug, name, description, category
Support --search <term> to filter by keyword
Support --category <name> to filter by category
Support --json flag to output raw JSON (for programmatic use by agents)
Human-readable table format by default (using picocolors for styling)
Clear error message when API request fails
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Add picocolors as dependency. Default output is human-friendly table; --json outputs raw API response.

userStories.title: List installed skills command
userStories.description: As an agent operator, I want to see what skills are currently installed locally or globally.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement src/skill/commands/list.ts handler for installed mode (no --remote flag)
purr skill list reads local lock file and displays installed skills
purr skill list --global reads global lock file
Each skill shows: slug, name, installed_at, canonical_path, list of agents with their paths
Support --json flag for programmatic output
Show friendly empty state message when no skills are installed
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reads lock file via lock.ts module. Same list.ts file handles both --remote and installed modes.

userStories.title: Install skill command (non-interactive, flags only)
userStories.description: As an agent operator, I want to install a skill by providing slug and agent via CLI flags.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement src/skill/commands/install.ts handler
purr skill install <slug> --agent <name> downloads and installs skill to specified agent
Support multiple --agent flags to install to multiple agents at once
Support --global to install to global scope (default is project-level local)
Support --copy to force copy mode instead of symlink
Support --force to overwrite if skill already installed
Verify SHA256 after download, abort with error on mismatch
Update lock file after successful install via upsertLockEntry()
Support --json flag for programmatic output (output install result as JSON)
Error if skill slug not found on marketplace (404)
Error if specified --agent name not recognized
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the non-interactive path. All required info provided via flags. Orchestrates: api.downloadSkill() -> installer.installSkill() -> lock.upsertLockEntry().

userStories.title: Install skill interactive mode (agent selection and skill search)
userStories.description: As an agent operator, I want interactive prompts when I don't provide all flags, so I can discover and select skills and agents interactively.
acceptanceCriteria: Add @clack/prompts as dependency
When purr skill install is run without <slug>: show interactive search prompt, call listSkills with user input, display results as selectable list
When purr skill install <slug> is run without --agent: call detectInstalled() to find available agents, show multi-select prompt for user to pick target agents
If no agents detected, show helpful message suggesting manual --agent flag
Interactive prompts are skipped entirely when all flags are provided (non-interactive path from US-008)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Extends install.ts from US-008. @clack/prompts provides select(), multiselect(), text() for interactive UI. Check if stdin is TTY before showing prompts.

userStories.title: Remove skill command
userStories.description: As an agent operator, I want to uninstall a skill to clean up unused skills.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement src/skill/commands/remove.ts handler
purr skill remove <slug> removes skill from all agents and canonical path
Support --agent <name> to remove only from a specific agent (keep others and canonical)
Support --global to remove from global scope
Support --yes to skip confirmation prompt
Without --yes, show confirmation prompt via @clack/prompts (list what will be deleted)
Without <slug>, show interactive list of installed skills to select from
When removing from specific agent: update agent_installs in lock entry, keep canonical if other agents remain
When removing last agent: delete canonical directory and remove lock entry entirely
Support --json flag for programmatic output
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Uses installer.uninstallSkill() for file cleanup and lock.removeLockEntry() / lock.upsertLockEntry() for state. Interactive mode via @clack/prompts.

userStories.title: Unit tests for skill modules
userStories.description: As a developer, I need tests for the core skill modules to ensure correctness.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/__tests__/skill-lock.test.ts: test readLock returns empty on missing file, writeLock + readLock roundtrip, upsertLockEntry adds and updates, removeLockEntry removes
Create src/__tests__/skill-agents.test.ts: test agent registry has expected agents, getAgent returns correct agent, detectInstalled returns array
Create src/__tests__/skill-installer.test.ts: test extractZip rejects path traversal attempts, SHA256 verification passes on correct hash and fails on mismatch
All tests pass with bun run test
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use vitest (already in devDependencies). Mock filesystem for lock tests. Use a small test ZIP buffer for installer tests.
